fundamentals.name: Oklo Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Oklo Inc. develops fission power plants to provide energy at scale to customers in the United States. The company offers Aurora Powerhouse, which is designed to produce between 15 and up to 75 megawatts of electricity. It is also commercializing nuclear fuel recycling and fuel fabrication technology that can convert used nuclear fuel into usable fuel for its reactors. The company was formerly known as AltC Acquisition Corp. and changed its name to Oklo Inc. in May 2024. Oklo Inc. was founded in 2013 and is headquartered in Santa Clara, California.

judgment.summary: Early-stage company with significant growth potential but currently unprofitable and with no revenue.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by -65.1%; next earnings date is 2026-08-10.
positives: Strong cash position of $2.21B provides ample runway.
Low debt/equity ratio of 0.10 indicates minimal leverage.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target suggesting significant upside potential.
High current and quick ratios (59.93, 59.73) suggest excellent short-term liquidity.
negatives: Gross, operating, and profit margins are all 0.0%, indicating no current profitability.
Negative return on equity (-8.9%) and assets (-7.2%) show poor efficiency.
Forward P/E of -78.52 and negative free cash flow of $-100.00M highlight current unprofitability.
Last quarter's EPS missed estimates by a significant margin (-65.1%).

news.paragraph: Oklo is garnering attention with a volatile valuation story, amplified by a new AI Lab partnership and a recent "prediction" of a 75% surge this year. While the company is slated to participate in an upcoming energy investment conference, its core financials remain a point of discussion. The upcoming August earnings date will be key to seeing if the company can translate its potential into tangible results.
